CGAL::Orthtree.name: CGAL::Orthtree
CGAL::Orthtree.brief: A data structure using an axis-aligned hyperrectangle decomposition of dD space for efficient access and computation.
CGAL::Orthtree.detailed: It builds a hierarchy of nodes which subdivides the space. Each node represents an axis-aligned hyperrectangle region of space. The contents of nodes depend on the traits class, non-leaf nodes also contain $2^{dim}$ other nodes which further subdivide the region. CGAL::Quadtree CGAL::Octree GeomTraits must be a model of OrthtreeTraits or OrthtreeTraitsWithData .

members.brief: Dimension of the tree.

members.name: Self
members.brief: Self alias for convenience.

members.name: Local_coordinates
members.brief: Set of bits representing this node's relationship to its parent.
members.detailed: Equivalent to an array of Booleans, where index[0] is whether x is greater, index[1] is whether y is greater, index[2] is whether z is greater, and so on for higher dimensions if needed. Used to represent a node's relationship to the center of its parent.

members.name: Global_coordinates
members.brief: Coordinates representing this node's relationship with the rest of the tree.
members.detailed: Each value (x, y, z, ...) of global coordinates is calculated by doubling the parent's global coordinates and adding the local coordinates.

members.name: Split_predicate
members.brief: A predicate that determines whether a node must be split when refining a tree.
members.definition: using CGAL::Orthtree< GeomTraits >::Split_predicate = std::function<bool(Node_index, const Self&)>

members.name: Node_index_range
members.brief: A model of ForwardRange whose value type is Node_index .

members.name: Property_map
members.brief: A model of LvaluePropertyMap with Node_index as key type and T as value type.

members.name: degree
members.brief: Degree of the tree (number of children of non-leaf nodes).

members.name: Orthtree
members.brief: constructs an orthtree for a traits instance.
members.detailed: The constructed orthtree has a root node with no children, containing the contents determined by Construct_root_node_contents from the traits class. That root node has a bounding box determined by Construct_root_node_bbox from the traits class, which typically encloses its contents. This single-node orthtree is valid and compatible with all orthtree functionality, but any performance benefits are unlikely to be realized until refine() is called. traits the traits object.

members.name: Orthtree
members.brief: constructs an orthtree from a set of arguments provided to the traits constructor

members.name: Orthtree
members.brief: copy constructor

members.name: Orthtree
members.brief: move constructor

members.name: refine
members.brief: recursively subdivides the orthtree until it meets the given criteria.
members.detailed: The split predicate should return true if a leaf node should be split and false otherwise. This function may be called several times with different predicates: in that case, nodes already split are left unaltered, while nodes that were not split and for which split_predicate returns true are split. split_predicate determines whether or not a leaf node needs to be subdivided.

members.name: refine
members.brief: convenience overload that refines an orthtree using a maximum depth and maximum number of contained elements in a node as split predicate.
members.detailed: This is equivalent to calling refine(Orthtrees::Maximum_depth_and_maximum_contained_elements(max_depth, bucket_size)) . The refinement is stopped as soon as one of the conditions is violated: if a node contains more elements than bucket_size but is already at max_depth , it is not split. Similarly, a node that is at a depth smaller than max_depth but already contains fewer elements than bucket_size , it is not split. This convenience method is only appropriate for trees with traits classes where Node_data is a model of Range . RandomAccessRange is suggested for performance. max_depth deepest a tree is allowed to be (nodes at this depth will not be split). bucket_size maximum number of items a node is allowed to contain.

members.name: grade
members.brief: refines the orthtree such that the difference of depth between two immediate neighbor leaves is never more than 1.
members.detailed: This is done only by adding nodes, nodes are never removed.

members.name: traits
members.brief: provides direct read-only access to the tree traits.

members.name: root
members.brief: provides access to the root node, and by extension the rest of the tree.

members.name: depth
members.brief: returns the deepest level reached by a leaf node in this tree (root being level 0).

members.name: traverse
members.brief: constructs a node index range using a tree-traversal function.
members.detailed: This method allows iteration over the nodes of the tree with a user-selected order (preorder, postorder, leaves-only, etc.). Traversal a model of OrthtreeTraversal traversal class defining the traversal strategy a ForwardRange over the node indices of the tree

members.name: locate
members.brief: finds the leaf node which contains a particular point in space.
members.detailed: Traverses the orthtree and finds the leaf cell that has a domain enclosing the point passed. The point passed must be within the region enclosed by the orthtree (bbox of the root node). The point is contained in the lower cell of each direction if its coordinate is lower than the center. point query point. the index of the node which contains the point.

members.name: nearest_k_neighbors
members.brief: finds the k nearest neighbors of the point query .
members.detailed: Nearest neighbors are outputted in order of increasing distance to query . OutputIterator a model of OutputIterator that accepts GeomTraits::Node_data_element objects. query query point k number of neighbors to find output output iterator Nearest neighbor searches requires GeomTraits to be a model of CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits .
members.argsstring: (const Point &query, std::size_t k, OutputIterator output) const

members.name: neighbors_within_radius
members.brief: finds the elements in the sphere query .
members.detailed: Elements are outputted in order of increasing distance to the center of the sphere. OutputIterator a model of OutputIterator that accepts GeomTraits::Node_data_element objects. query query sphere output output iterator Nearest neighbor searches requires GeomTraits to be a model of CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits .

members.name: nearest_k_neighbors_within_radius
members.brief: finds at most k elements within a specific radius that are nearest to the center of the sphere query : if query does not contain at least k elements, only contained elements will be returned.
members.detailed: This function is useful when the user already knows how sparse the elements are, or if they do not care about elements that are too far away. Setting a small radius may have performance benefits. OutputIterator must be a model of OutputIterator that accepts GeomTraits::Node_data_element query the region to search within k the number of elements to find output the output iterator to add the found elements to (in order of increasing distance) Nearest neighbor searches requires GeomTraits to be a model of CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits .
members.argsstring: (const Sphere &query, std::size_t k, OutputIterator output) const

members.detailed: This function finds all the intersecting leaf nodes and writes their indices to the output iterator. Requires the Traits class to provide the functor via has_on_bounded_side_object() with an operator: bool operator()(Traits::Sphere_d, Traits::Bbox_d) Kernel::HasOnUnboundedSide_2 and Kernel::HasOnUnboundedSide_3 are compatible concepts for dimensions 2 and 3. OutputIterator a model of OutputIterator that accepts Node_index types center the center of the ball squared_radius the squared radius of the ball output output iterator. the output iterator after writing

Query the primitive class (e.g., sphere, ray) OutputIterator a model of OutputIterator that accepts Node_index types IntersectionFunctor a functor that is invoked on each node while traversing the tree to determine intersection must be of type bool(const Query&, const Traits::Bbox_d&) query the intersecting primitive. output output iterator. func the intersection functor. the output iterator after writing

members.name: operator==
members.brief: compares the topology of the orthtree with that of rhs .
members.detailed: Trees may be considered equivalent even if they have different contents. Equivalent trees must have the same root bounding box and the same node structure. rhs the other orthtree true if the trees have the same topology, and false otherwise

members.name: descendant
members.brief: retrieves an arbitrary descendant of the node specified by node .
members.detailed: Convenience function to avoid the need to call orthtree.child(orthtree.child(node, 0), 1) . Each index in indices specifies which child to enter as descending the tree from node down. Indices are evaluated in the order they appear as parameters, so descendant(root, 0, 1) returns the second child of the first child of the root. node the node to descend indices the integer indices specifying the descent to perform the index of the specified descendant node

members.name: node
members.brief: convenience function for retrieving arbitrary nodes.
members.detailed: Equivalent to tree.descendant(tree.root(), indices...) . indices the integer indices specifying the descent to perform, starting from the root the index of the specified node

members.name: next_sibling
members.brief: finds the next sibling in the parent of the node specified by the index n .
members.detailed: Traverses the tree in increasing order of local index (e.g., 000, 001, 010, etc.) n the index of the node to find the sibling of the index of the next sibling of n if n is not the last node in its parent, otherwise std::nullopt .

members.name: next_sibling_up
members.brief: finds the next sibling of the parent of the node specified by n if it exists.
members.detailed: n the index node to find the sibling up of. The index of the next sibling of the parent of n if n is not the root and its parent has a sibling, otherwise nothing.

members.name: deepest_first_child
members.brief: finds the leaf node reached when descending the tree and always choosing child 0.
members.detailed: This is the starting point of a depth-first traversal. n the index of the node to find the deepest first child of. the index of the deepest first child of node n.

members.name: first_child_at_depth
members.brief: finds node reached when descending the tree to a depth d and always choosing child 0.
members.detailed: Similar to deepest_first_child() , but does go to a fixed depth. n the index of the node to find the d th first child of. d the depth to descend to. the index of the d th first child, nothing if the tree is not deep enough.

members.name: split
members.brief: splits a node into subnodes.
members.detailed: Only leaf nodes should be split. When a node is split it is no longer a leaf node. The full set of degree children are constructed automatically, and their values are set. Contents of this node are not propagated automatically, this is responsibility of the distribute_node_contents_object in the traits class. n index of the node to split

members.name: barycenter
members.brief: returns the center point of a node.
members.detailed: n index of the node to find the center point for the center point of node n

members.name: adjacent_node
members.brief: finds the directly adjacent node in a specific direction
members.detailed: direction.to_ulong < 2 * dimension Adjacent nodes are found according to several properties: adjacent nodes may be larger than the seek node, but never smaller a node has at most 2 * dimension different adjacent nodes (in 3D: left, right, up, down, front, back) adjacent nodes are not required to be leaf nodes Here's a diagram demonstrating the concept for a quadtree: +---------------+---------------+ | | | | | | | | | | A | | | | | | | | | | | +-------+-------+---+---+-------+ | | | | | | | A | (S) +---A---+ | | | | | | | +---+---+-------+---+---+-------+ | | | | | | +---+---+ A | | | | | | | | | +---+---+-------+-------+-------+ (S) : Seek node A : Adjacent node Note how the top adjacent node is larger than the seek node. The right adjacent node is the same size, even though it contains further subdivisions. This implementation returns the adjacent node if it's found. If there is no adjacent node in that direction, it returns a null node. n index of the node to find a neighbor of direction which way to find the adjacent node relative to this one. Each successive bit selects the direction for the corresponding dimension: for an octree in 3D, 010 means: negative direction in X, position direction in Y, negative direction in Z. the index of the adjacent node if it exists, nothing otherwise.

members.name: adjacent_node
members.brief: equivalent to adjacent_node() , with an adjacency direction rather than a bitset.
members.detailed: n index of the node to find a neighbor of adjacency which way to find the adjacent node relative to this one

members.brief: determines whether a pair of subtrees have the same topology.
members.detailed: lhsNode index of a node in lhsTree lhsTree an Orthtree rhsNode index of a node in rhsTree rhsTree another Orthtree true if lhsNode and rhsNode have the same topology, false otherwise
members.argsstring: (Node_index lhsNode, const Self &lhsTree, Node_index rhsNode, const Self &rhsTree)

members.name: is_topology_equal
members.brief: helper function for calling is_topology_equal() on the root nodes of two trees.
members.detailed: lhs an Orthtree rhs another Orthtree true if lhs and rhs have the same topology, and false otherwise

members.name: Adjacency
members.brief: Adjacency type.
members.detailed: This type is used to identify adjacency directions with easily understandable keywords (left, right, up, down, ...) and is thus mainly useful in 2D and 3D. In higher dimensions, such keywords do not exist and this type is simply an integer. Conversions from this integer to bitsets still work but do not provide any user-friendly API for adjacency selection. Two directions along each axis in Cartesian space, relative to a node. Directions are mapped to numbers as 3-bit integers in the 3D case or as 2-bit integers in the 2D case. In the 3d case the numbers 6 and 7 are not used because there are only 6 different directions. The first two bits indicate the axis (00 = x, 01 = y, 10 = z), the third bit indicates the direction along that axis (0 = -, 1 = +). The following diagram and table showing the 3D case may be a useful reference (2D case is identical with one dimension less): 3 * | * 4 | / y+ |/ * 0 *------+------* 1 | /| | / | +-----* x+ 5 * | / * 2 / * z+ This lookup table may also be helpful: Direction bitset number Enum -x 000 0 LEFT +x 001 1 RIGHT -y 010 2 DOWN +y 011 3 UP -z 100 4 BACK +z 101 5 FRONT

CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph.name: CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph
CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph.brief: Traits class for the Orthtree class to be used to construct a 3D octree around a triangulated surface mesh.
CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph.detailed: Each node of the octree will store all the faces of the mesh intersected by its bounding box. The subdivision of the octree is controlled by the nested class Orthtree_traits_face_graph::Split_predicate_node_min_extent to which the minimal extent of a node should be provided. TriangleMesh a model of FaceListGraph with all faces being triangles VertexPointMap a property map associating points to the vertices of TriangleMesh Is model of OrthtreeTraitsWithData

CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point.name: CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point
CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point.brief: Traits class for defining an orthtree of points using the class CGAL::Orthtree .
CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point.detailed: GeomTraits model of Kernel . PointRange must be a model of Range whose value type is the key type of PointMap and whose iterator type is model of RandomAccessIterator PointMap must be a model of ReadablePropertyMap whose value type is a point type from GeomTraits matching the current dimension dimension the dimension of the ambient Euclidean space. The input point set is not copied. It is used directly and is rearranged by the Orthtree . Altering the point range after creating the orthtree will leave it in an invalid state. Is model of CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits CGAL::Octree CGAL::Quadtree CGAL::Orthtree_traits_base <GeomTraits, dimension>

CGAL::Orthtrees::Leaves_traversal.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Leaves_traversal
CGAL::Orthtrees::Leaves_traversal.brief: A class used for performing a traversal on leaves only.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Leaves_traversal.detailed: Tree an instance of Orthtree All non-leaf nodes are ignored. Is model of OrthtreeTraversal

CGAL::Orthtrees::Level_traversal.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Level_traversal
CGAL::Orthtrees::Level_traversal.brief: A class used for performing a traversal of a specific depth level.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Level_traversal.detailed: Tree an instance of Orthtree All tree nodes at another depth are ignored. If the selected depth is higher than the maximum depth of the orthtree, no node will be traversed. Is model of OrthtreeTraversal

members.name: Level_traversal
members.brief: constructs a depth -level traversal.

CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_contained_eleme.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_contained_elements
CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_contained_eleme.brief: A class used to choose when a node should be split depending on the number of contained elements.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_contained_eleme.detailed: This is a bucket size predicate that considers a node should be split if it contains more than a certain number of items. This split predicate is only appropriate for trees with traits classes which are models of OrthtreeTraitsWithData and where Node_data is a model of Range . RandomAccessRange is suggested for performance.

members.name: Maximum_contained_elements
members.brief: creates a predicate based on the number of contained elements.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true if the node with index i should be split, false otherwise.
members.argsstring: (typename Orthtree< GeomTraits >::Node_index i, const Orthtree< GeomTraits > &tree) const

CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth
CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth.brief: A class used to choose when a node should be split depending on the depth.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth.detailed: This predicate makes a node be split if its depth is lower than a certain limit.

members.name: Maximum_depth
members.brief: creates a maximum depth predicate.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true if the node with index i should be split, false otherwise.
members.argsstring: (typename Orthtree< GeomTraits >::Node_index i, const Orthtree< GeomTraits > &tree) const

CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth_and_maxim.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth_and_maximum_contained_elements
CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth_and_maxim.brief: A class used to choose when a node should be split depending on the depth and the number of contained elements.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth_and_maxim.detailed: This predicate makes a node split if it contains more than a certain number of items and if its depth is lower than a certain limit. The refinement is stopped as soon as one of the conditions is violated: if a node has more elements than bucket_size but is already at max_depth , it is not split. Similarly, a node that is at a depth smaller than max_depth but already has fewer elements than bucket_size , it is not split. This split predicate is only appropriate for trees with traits classes which are models of OrthtreeTraitsWithData and where Node_data is a model of Range . RandomAccessRange is suggested for performance.

members.name: Maximum_depth_and_maximum_contained_elements
members.brief: creates a predicate using maximum depth or bucket size.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: returns true if the node with index i should be split, false otherwise.
members.argsstring: (typename Orthtree< GeomTraits >::Node_index i, const Orthtree< GeomTraits > &tree) const

CGAL::Orthtrees::Postorder_traversal.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Postorder_traversal
CGAL::Orthtrees::Postorder_traversal.brief: A class used for performing a postorder traversal.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Postorder_traversal.detailed: Tree an instance of Orthtree A postorder traversal starts from the leaves towards the root. Is model of OrthtreeTraversal

CGAL::Orthtrees::Preorder_traversal.name: CGAL::Orthtrees::Preorder_traversal
CGAL::Orthtrees::Preorder_traversal.brief: A class used for performing a preorder traversal.
CGAL::Orthtrees::Preorder_traversal.detailed: Tree an instance of Orthtree A preorder traversal starts from the root towards the leaves. Is model of OrthtreeTraversal

CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits.name: CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits
CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits.brief: Refinement of the OrthtreeTraitsWithData concept, adding requirements for the traits class of a CGAL::Orthtree in order to supports nearest-neighbor searching.
CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits.detailed: Nearest neighbor searches expect a tree where Node_data is a model of ForwardRange . The leaf nodes of the tree represent an exclusive partition of the elements contained in the tree. This means that no element should be contained by more than one node. Refines OrthtreeTraitsWithData Has models CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap, DimensionTag>

members.name: Sphere_d
members.brief: Sphere Type used for the shrinking-sphere approach for neighbor queries; needs to be copy assignable.

members.name: Node_data
members.brief: The data type contained by each node; must be a model of ForwardRange in addition to default constructible, copy constructible and copy assignable.

members.name: Node_data_element
members.brief: An element of the Node_data list-like type.
members.detailed: Must be constructible from the value type of a Node_data::iterator . Typically the same as that type, but can also be an std::reference_wrapper<> if the type is not copyable.

members.detailed: Provides the operator: FT operator()(const Node_data_element &, const Point_d&)

members.detailed: Provides the operator: Sphere_d operator()(const Point_d&, const FT&)

members.brief: Functor with an operator that provides the center of a Sphere_d .
members.detailed: Provides the operator: Point_d operator()(const Sphere_d &)

members.name: Compute_squared_radius_d
members.brief: Functor with an operator that provides the squared radius of a Sphere_d .
members.detailed: Provides the operator: FT operator()(const Sphere_d &)

OrthtreeTraits.name: OrthtreeTraits
OrthtreeTraits.brief: The concept OrthtreeTraits defines the requirements for the template parameter of the CGAL::Orthtree class.
OrthtreeTraits.detailed: Has models CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap, dimension > CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph <PolygonMesh, VPM> CGAL::Orthtree_traits_base <K, dimension >

members.name: Node_index
members.brief: An integer type for nodes.

members.name: FT
members.brief: The number type of the Cartesian coordinates of types Point_d .

members.name: Point_d
members.brief: Point type.

members.name: Bbox_d
members.brief: Bounding box type. Must be constructible from a pair of Point_d objects.

members.name: Cartesian_const_iterator_d
members.brief: A random access iterator type to enumerate the Cartesian coordinates of a point of type Point_d .

members.name: Adjacency
members.brief: Integral number type which can take on values indicating adjacency directions.
members.detailed: Must be able to take on values ranging from 0 to the number of faces of the (hyper)rectangle type, equivalent to 2 * D. Specify the adjacency directions

members.name: Construct_root_node_bbox
members.brief: Functor with an operator to create the bounding box of the root node.
members.detailed: Provides the operator: Bbox_d operator()() The bounding box must enclose all elements contained by the tree. It may be tight-fitting. The orthtree constructor produces a bounding box surrounding this region. For traits which assign no data to each node, this can be defined to return a fixed region.

members.name: Construct_point_d
members.brief: Functor with an operator to construct a Point_d from an initializer list of type FT .
members.detailed: Provides the operator: Point_d operator()(arg1, arg2,...) For trees which use a different kernel for the bounding box type, the return type of this functor must match the kernel used by the bounding box type and not that of the contents.

members.name: dimension
members.brief: Dimension.

members.name: construct_root_node_bbox_object
members.brief: constructs an object of type Construct_root_node_bbox .

members.name: construct_point_d_object
members.brief: constructs an object of type Construct_point_d .

OrthtreeTraitsWithData.name: OrthtreeTraitsWithData
OrthtreeTraitsWithData.brief: The concept OrthtreeTraitsWithData defines the requirements for the template parameter of the CGAL::Orthtree class for a node type that stores data.
OrthtreeTraitsWithData.detailed: Refines OrthtreeTraits Has models CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap, dimension> CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph <PolygonMesh, VPM> CGAL::Orthtree_traits_base <K, dimension>

members.name: Node_data
members.brief: The data type contained by each node.
members.detailed: Must be default constructible, copy constructible and copy assignable.

members.name: Construct_root_node_contents
members.brief: Functor which initializes elements contained by the root node.
members.detailed: Each node of a tree has an associated Node_data value. This functor initializes the Node_data of the root node. It takes no arguments, and returns an instance of Node_data . Provides the operator: Node_data operator()() Typically, the Node_data of the root node contains all the elements in the tree. For a tree in which each node contains a span (such as std::span() ) this function would return the span containing all items.

members.name: Distribute_node_contents
members.brief: Functor which fills the contents of the nodes children.
members.detailed: Provides the operator: void operator()(Node_index, Orthtree<Traits>&, const Point_d&) The functor is called during refinement of the Orthtree on a node after it has been split. The purpose of the functor is to distribute the Node_data , accessible via tree.data() , to the data of the nodes children, accessible via tree.children() . The first parameter is the Node_index of the node. The second parameter provides the instance of the Orthtree and the last parameter is the barycenter of the node which will be used as shared corner amongst the children of the node. For a tree in which each node contains a span, this may mean rearranging the contents of the original node and producing spans containing a subset of its contents for each of its children. For compatibility with locate, the center of the node is considered to be part of the upper half.

members.name: construct_root_node_contents_object
members.brief: constructs an object of type Construct_root_node_contents .

members.name: distribute_node_contents_object
members.brief: constructs an object of type Distribute_node_contents .

PkgOrthtreeRef.name: PkgOrthtreeRef
PkgOrthtreeRef.detailed: Jackson Campolattaro, Simon Giraudot, Cédric Portaneri, Tong Zhao, and Pierre Alliez The Orthtree package provides a data structure that subdivides space, with specializations for 2D (Quadtree) and 3D (Octree), along with a collection of algorithms for operating on these structures. Introduced in: CGAL 5.3 BibTeX: cgal:cpz-o-26a License: GPL Classified Reference Pages Concepts OrthtreeTraits OrthtreeTraitsWithData OrthtreeTraversal CollectionPartitioningOrthtreeTraits Classes CGAL::Quadtree <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap> CGAL::Octree <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap> CGAL::Orthtree <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap> Traits CGAL::Orthtree_traits <GeomTraits, dimension> CGAL::Orthtree_traits_point <GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap, dimension> CGAL::Orthtree_traits_base <GeomTraits> CGAL::Orthtree_traits_face_graph <TriangleMesh, VertexPointMap> Split Predicates CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_contained_elements CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth CGAL::Orthtrees::Maximum_depth_and_maximum_contained_elements Traversal CGAL::Orthtrees::Preorder_traversal CGAL::Orthtrees::Postorder_traversal CGAL::Orthtrees::Leaves_traversal CGAL::Orthtrees::Level_traversal

members.name: Octree
members.brief: Alias that specializes the Orthtree class to a 3D octree storing 3D points.
members.detailed: GeomTraits a model of Kernel PointRange a model of Range whose value type is the key type of PointMap and whose iterator type is a model of RandomAccessIterator PointMap a model of ReadablePropertyMap whose value type is GeomTraits::Point_3 cubic_nodes Boolean to enforce cubic nodes
members.return_type: Orthtree< Orthtree_traits_point< GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap, cubic_nodes, 3 > >

members.name: Quadtree
members.brief: Alias that specializes the Orthtree class to a 2D quadtree storing 2D points.
members.detailed: GeomTraits must be a model of Kernel PointRange must be a model of Range whose value type is the key type of PointMap PointMap must be a model of ReadablePropertyMap whose value type is GeomTraits::Point_2 square_nodes Boolean to enforce square nodes
members.return_type: Orthtree< Orthtree_traits_point< GeomTraits, PointRange, PointMap, squared_nodes, 2 > >
